questions.question: India's demographic profile on the eve of independence reflected the socio-economic backwardness imposed by colonial rule. Which of the following options best describes the health care and nutrition status on the eve of independence?
questions.option_1: Moderate life expectancy with declining birth and death rates
questions.option_2: High life expectancy with high birth rates and low death rate
questions.option_3: High life expectancy with low birth and death rates
questions.option_4: Low life expectancy with high birth and death rates

questions.question: During which phase and year was a steady population growth rate observed in India?
Which of the following is NOT a dimension measured by the Gender Inequality Index (GII)?
As per April 2025, Reserve Bank of India (RBI) allows flexibility in UPI transaction limits for in-person merchant payments, ranging from ₹2 lakh to _____. .
Which of the following banking facilities is/are operated by National Payments Corporation of India? 1. National Electronic Funds Transfer (NEFT) 2. Aadhaar Enabled Payment System 3. UPI Lite
Globalisation refers to the integration of economies worldwide. Which of the following was a major globalisation strategy adopted by the Indian government in 1991?
questions.option_1: Reducing trade barriers and encouraging foreign investment

questions.question: Till which year has the term of Chief Economic Adviser V Anantha Nageswaran been extended?

In which year did the government notify the New Public Sector Enterprise (PSE) Policy for Atma Nirbhar Bharat, that provides overall guidance on strategic disinvestment, and intends to minimise the presence of Government in PSEs across all sectors of economy?
Banking reforms were part of the 1991 economic reforms. Which of the following committees recommended banking sector liberalisation in 1991?
Which of the following statements is NOT correct regarding India after its adoption of new economic reforms in 1991?
questions.option_1: After the economic reforms, the growth of agriculture sector declined in the country.
questions.option_2: The 1991 reforms have caused a huge increase in public investment in the agriculture sector and increased its share in India’s GDP.
questions.option_3: Liberalisation of trade and investment regime was initiated in India post 1991 to increase international competitiveness of industrial production.
questions.option_4: The industrial sector does not show an enormous increase in the GDP contribution of India post 1991.

questions.question: In February 2025, Muthoot Finance has received RBI approval to open how many new branches across India, strengthening its presence in the gold loan market?
What was the overall unemployment rate in India as reported in the 6ᵗʰ Annual Employment-Unemployment Survey conducted by theLabourBureau?
The Indian telecom sector wasliberalisedunder which year’s Telecom Policy?
What was the labour force participation rate for males in India as reported in the 6ᵗʰ Annual Employment-Unemployment Survey conducted by the Labour Bureau?
In April 2025, NITI Aayog released a report titled "Automotive Industry – Powering India's Participation in Global Value Chains". What is the primary objective of this report?
questions.option_1: To compare India's automotive policies with those of neighbouring countries
questions.option_2: To propose strategies for enhancing India's role in global automotive value chains
questions.option_3: To assess the environmental impact of India's automotive sector
questions.option_4: To analyse India's automotive export performance over the past decade

questions.question: What was the budget allocation for the Ministry of Rural Development for the fiscal year 2023-24?
What was the unemployment rate for persons aged 15 years and above in rural India during the period 2022-23, according to the Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) annual report 2022-23?

In March 2025, NITI Aayog released the 'Trade Watch – Quarterly (July-September [Q2] FY25)' report. According to this report, which of the following commodities entered India's top ten imports due to rising infrastructure demand?
What was the estimated capital expenditure of the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways in India for the financial year 2024-25?
What was the poverty rate in India in 2019-21, according to the India National Multidimensional Poverty Index, 2023?
The Reserve Bank of India has its offices in how many locations in India, as of April 2025?
The Capital Adequacy Ratio, sometimes seen in news, is related to which of the following?
What percentage increase in cement production was recorded in FY24 due to the government’s infrastructure push?
What was the average annual growth rate of industries comprising mining, manufacturing and electricity during the post-reform period between 1991-92 and 2011-12?
In April 2025, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) adjusted its monetary policy stance in response to global economic uncertainties. What was the new stance adopted by the RBI?
